0.name: data-triage
0.description: Inventories the data pool, identifies the most recent annual filing / quarterly filing / transcript / deck, and issues a fail-fast verdict (Sufficient / Partial / Insufficient) before the rest of the pipeline runs.

1.name: disqualifier-scan
1.description: Scans the data pool for 8 evidence-based hard disqualifiers (audit qualification, going concern, promoter pledging, related-party transactions, repeated auditor changes, material restatements, regulatory enforcement, customer concentration without contracts, chronic negative operating cash flow). Triggering ANY locks the final verdict at "Low-quality business — avoid deeper work".

1.name: business-identity
1.description: Describes in plain English what the company actually does, expresses the revenue model as a simple formula, and classifies the business type. The foundational identity layer that downstream agents (value-chain, competitive-map) rely on.

1.name: segment-map
1.description: Maps the company's reportable segments — what each does, revenue and profit share, margin quality, capital intensity, cyclicality, and main risk. Identifies the dominant segment (largest revenue or profit contributor). Critical input for unit-economics and competitive-map.

1.name: customer-geography
1.description: Maps customer types and geographies — importance, evidence, risk — and flags concentration. Identifies whether the business depends on a small number of customers or geographies, and whether that's contractually secured or not.

1.name: external-dependency
1.description: Identifies the company's exposure to external variables (commodities, rates, FX, freight, policy, regulation, weather, geopolitics, consumer cycle, industrial cycle) and classifies the business as Company-controlled, Partly externally driven, or Mostly externally driven. Produces an external-dependency-risk score (higher = worse).

1.name: capital-allocation-governance
1.description: Scores 13 capital-allocation and governance signals — acquisition pattern, share count trajectory, dividends, capex, debt, related-party transactions, insider ownership, promoter pledging, auditor history, restatements, off-balance-sheet items, working capital, senior management turnover. Classifies as owner-operator discipline / standard / concerns / red flags.

2.name: unit-economics
2.description: Identifies the company's natural economic unit (per customer, per store, per ton, per loan, per AUM dollar, etc.) and tables out per-unit revenue, gross margin, contribution margin, acquisition cost, and payback. Reads segment-map output to identify the dominant unit. Decides whether each new unit creates or destroys value.

2.name: value-chain
2.description: Locates the company in its value chain — raw material supplier, component supplier, manufacturer, distributor, retailer, platform, service provider, or end-customer-facing — and assesses whether it controls its economics or gets squeezed by suppliers and customers.

2.name: business-quality
2.description: Scores the business across 11 quality factors — pricing power, recurring revenue, customer stickiness, margin stability, capital intensity, competitive intensity, industry rate-of-change / disruption risk, regulatory dependence, commodity dependence, cyclicality, disclosure quality. Reads segment-map and customer-geography outputs to inform several factors.

2.name: competitive-map
2.description: Identifies 2–3 of the most credible competitors for the company's dominant segment and profiles each. Provides the named-competitor anchor that the moat agent uses for its competitive economics comparison.

3.name: moat
3.description: Evaluates the company's competitive moat against named competitors from competitive-map. Builds the moat-source table (10 candidate moats), the competitive economics table (margins and ROIC vs peers), and a moat verdict with strength score.

4.name: red-flags-sweep
4.description: Catch-all sweep for red flags not already captured by upstream specialists. Reads outputs from disqualifier-scan, segment-map, customer-geography, business-quality, external-dependency, and capital-allocation-governance to avoid duplication. Surfaces only flags that are evidence-supported and not already prominent in upstream reports.

5.name: business-model-synthesis
5.description: Reads ALL upstream specialist outputs and composes the final business-model report — Abstract, Verdict block (with disqualifier check, 7 scores), Note to Final Synthesizer, and Simple Summary. Reconciles disagreements, applies the disqualifier verdict-lock rule, and surfaces what the scores MEAN.

0.name: earnings-data-triage
0.description: Inventories earnings-relevant data in the data pool. Checks for latest financials, quarterly filings, transcripts, guidance, consensus, estimate revisions, and cash flow data. Issues Sufficient / Partial / Insufficient verdict before the rest of the Earnings module runs.

1.name: historical-financials
1.description: Builds the historical financial baseline — 3–5 year annual table, latest quarterly trend, seasonality analysis, and YoY/QoQ trends. Covers revenue, gross profit, EBITDA, EBIT, EPS, margins, CFO, FCF, capex, working capital, and leverage. Foundation layer for all downstream earnings agents.

1.name: guidance-consensus
1.description: Compares management guidance against Capital IQ consensus and tracks estimate revision momentum (90/60/30 days). Decides whether the market's bar is low, fair, high, or unknown. Runs in Layer 1 without upstream dependencies.

2.name: revenue-drivers
2.description: Identifies what moves revenue — volume, price, mix, utilization, backlog, ARPU, subscribers, shipments, commodity prices, store count, and segment mix. Decomposes by segment when business-model segment-map is available. Reads historical-financials upstream for the baseline.

2.name: margin-drivers
2.description: Identifies what moves margins — input costs, pricing, mix, utilization, freight, energy, wages, FX, operating leverage, depreciation, one-offs, and segment mix. Decomposes by segment when business-model segment-map is available. Reads historical-financials upstream for the baseline.

2.name: earnings-quality
2.description: Checks whether earnings are clean and repeatable. Covers cash conversion, EBITDA-to-FCF bridge, working capital trends, non-GAAP adjustments, one-off items, accruals, and inventory/channel risk. Reads historical-financials upstream for the baseline.

3.name: beat-miss-setup
3.description: Determines what could cause the next quarter (or next two quarters) to beat or miss expectations. Combines revenue drivers, margin drivers, guidance/consensus bar, seasonality, and historical beat/miss patterns. The most forward-looking agent in the earnings module.

3.name: earnings-sensitivity
3.description: Identifies the 3–7 variables with the highest earnings sensitivity and estimates directional bull/bear EPS or EBITDA impact where evidence allows. Uses the structured sensitivity table format. Reads historical-financials, revenue-drivers, and margin-drivers upstream.

3.name: earnings-red-flags
3.description: Catch-all earnings red-flag specialist. Reviews all upstream earnings outputs and flags hidden risks, contradictions, weak evidence, consensus traps, quality traps, and setup breakers before final earnings synthesis.

4.name: earnings-synthesis
4.description: Reads ALL upstream earnings module outputs and produces the final Earnings module report — Abstract, Verdict block (with 6 scores), Specialist roll-up, Reconciliation, Note to Final Synthesizer, and Simple Summary. The master synthesizer at .claude/agents/synthesizer.md reads this output.

0.name: solvency-data-triage
0.description: Inventories balance-sheet and debt data in the data pool. Checks for the balance sheet, debt notes, maturity schedule, covenant disclosures, cash flow, committed facilities, contingencies, and ratings. Issues Sufficient / Partial / Insufficient verdict before the rest of the Balance-Sheet-Survival module runs.

1.name: capital-structure-and-leverage
1.description: Builds the full debt stack (short/long-term, secured/unsecured, senior/sub, bonds/loans/revolver, finance leases), adds lease/pension/preferred obligations, and computes gross and net leverage with the trend. The foundation every other solvency agent uses.

2.name: maturity-wall-and-refinancing
2.description: Lays out the debt maturity schedule by year, computes weighted-average maturity and the share due within 12/24/36 months, maps fixed vs floating exposure and coupon vs current market rates, and assesses refinancing risk and the cost step-up.

2.name: liquidity-runway
2.description: Measures committed liquidity (cash + liquid investments + undrawn committed facilities) against near-term cash obligations (maturities, interest, maintenance capex, committed dividends) and expresses the runway in months, with a sources-and-uses bridge.

2.name: coverage-and-covenants
2.description: Computes interest and fixed-charge coverage ratios and maps each maintenance covenant — threshold, current actual, and headroom to a breach. Identifies the tightest covenant and what would trip it.

2.name: off-balance-sheet-and-contingencies
2.description: Surfaces obligations that sit outside or beneath the headline debt line — operating leases, pension/OPEB underfunding, guarantees, standby letters of credit, securitizations/factoring, purchase commitments, and litigation/tax contingencies — recording both the recognized liability and the maximum exposure.

3.name: downside-stress-test
3.description: The survival test. Applies explicit EBITDA haircuts (−30/−40/−60%, or cyclically calibrated) and recomputes leverage, coverage, covenant headroom (breach Y/N), and the 12-month liquidity gap at each, then identifies the EBITDA decline at which covenants break or liquidity runs out.

4.name: balance-sheet-survival-synthesis
4.description: Reads ALL upstream balance-sheet-survival outputs and produces the final module report — Abstract, Verdict block (with 6 scores and the survival read), Specialist roll-up, Reconciliation, Score Cap application, Note to Final Synthesizer, and Simple Summary. The master synthesizer at .claude/agents/synthesizer.md reads this output and defers its "Balance Sheet and Survival Test" section to it.

0.name: governance-data-triage
0.description: Inventories management- and governance-relevant data in the data pool. Checks for the proxy/compensation disclosure, ownership and insider-transaction data, board composition, related-party disclosure, shareholder letters, and capital-allocation history. Issues Sufficient / Partial / Insufficient verdict before the rest of the Management-Governance module runs.

1.name: management-and-track-record
1.description: Establishes who runs the company (CEO/CFO tenure, background), management stability/turnover, and the stated strategy — then judges the record by comparing prior promises and guidance to actual outcomes. The foundation the other governance specialists build on.

2.name: capital-allocation-scorecard
2.description: Scores the historical capital-allocation record over 3–5 years — M&A (price paid vs value/synergies delivered), buybacks (dollars and average price vs value), dividends (coverage/sustainability), organic reinvestment (incremental ROIC), and debt — judged on per-share outcomes. Deepens the business-model capital-allocation quick-read.

2.name: incentives-and-compensation
2.description: Dissects executive compensation — the base/bonus/LTIP mix, the actual performance metrics and weights that pay out, and whether they reward per-share value (ROIC/EPS/TSR) or size (revenue/absolute EBITDA/deal count). Assesses pay magnitude vs performance and peers.

2.name: ownership-and-insider-behavior
2.description: Maps insider/promoter ownership and skin in the game, recent net insider buying vs selling, share pledging, and the control structure (dual-class, super-voting, shareholder blocs with board rights). Distinguishes shares bought with conviction from shares merely granted.

2.name: board-and-shareholder-rights
2.description: Assesses board independence, tenure and refreshment, related-party transactions, takeover defenses, and voting rights — judging how well minority shareholders are protected from entrenchment and self-dealing.

2.name: candor-and-disclosure-quality
2.description: Judges whether management tells the truth in good times and bad — owning misses vs burying them, the aggressiveness of non-GAAP adjustments (cross-checked against earnings quality), the transparency of segment/KPI disclosure, and promotional vs conservative tone.

3.name: management-governance-synthesis
3.description: Reads ALL upstream management-governance outputs and produces the final module report — Abstract, Verdict block (with 8 scores and a stewardship verdict), Specialist roll-up, Reconciliation, Score Cap application, Note to Final Synthesizer, and Simple Summary. The master synthesizer reads this as a module chapter and treats its governance verdict as primary (superseding the business-model quick-read).

0.name: valuation-data-triage
0.description: Inventories valuation-relevant data in the data pool. Checks for current price, share count, capital structure, forward estimates, historical and peer multiples, segment data, and cash flow. Issues Sufficient / Partial / Insufficient verdict before the rest of the Valuation module runs.

1.name: price-and-capital-structure
1.description: Establishes the valuation anchor — current price, diluted share count, market cap, and the full market-cap → enterprise-value bridge (debt, cash, minority interest, preferred). Solves the recurring "no current price" gap or flags it hard. Foundation that every other valuation agent uses.

2.name: multiples-own-history
2.description: Values the stock against its OWN trading-multiple history (3–5 year bands for P/E, EV/EBITDA, EV/EBIT, EV/Sales, P/B, FCF yield, dividend yield). Decides whether the company is re-rated or de-rated versus its own past, and the implied value from reverting to its own mean.

2.name: relative-valuation-peers
2.description: Values the company against named peers. Builds a peer comp table (P/E, EV/EBITDA, EV/EBIT, EV/Sales, FCF yield, growth, margins, ROIC), computes the premium/discount to the peer median, judges whether that gap is warranted given quality/moat/leverage, and derives an implied value from peer multiples.

2.name: intrinsic-dcf
2.description: Builds an intrinsic value — an FCFF DCF for operating/commodity businesses (every assumption sourced, terminal value disclosed as a % of EV, WACC × terminal-growth sensitivity grid), branching to DDM / residual-income (financials) or NAV (REITs) per the Business-Type Method Map. Never forces an FCFF DCF onto a business where it is meaningless.

2.name: reverse-dcf
2.description: Backs out what the current price implies. Holds the discount rate and horizon fixed and solves for the growth/margin the market is pricing in, then judges whether those implied expectations are achievable against earnings-module evidence and the company's own history.

2.name: sum-of-the-parts
2.description: Values each reportable segment separately on a defensible segment-level multiple tied to a named comparable, sums to a gross enterprise value, bridges to equity (net debt, corporate costs, minority, investments), and derives a per-share breakup value. Mandatory for multi-segment businesses; collapses to the consolidated read for single-segment ones.

3.name: scenario-and-fair-value
3.description: Triangulates the five valuation lenses (own-history multiples, peers, DCF, reverse-DCF, SOTP) into a single base-case fair value plus bull/base/bear fair-value LEVELS, states the margin of safety to the bear case, and checks the warranted multiple. Produces levels only — probabilities and risk/reward belong to the master synthesizer.

4.name: valuation-synthesis
4.description: Reads ALL upstream valuation module outputs and produces the final Valuation module report — Abstract, Verdict block (with 6 scores and a fair-value range), Specialist roll-up, Reconciliation, Score Cap application, Note to Final Synthesizer, and Simple Summary. The master synthesizer at .claude/agents/synthesizer.md reads this output and defers its valuation section to it.

0.name: catalyst-data-triage
0.description: Inventories forward-looking, scheduled-event data in the data pool and the run's upstream module outputs (results dates, guidance, debt maturities, AGM notices, regulatory/clinical timelines, capital-return policy). Issues Sufficient / Partial / Insufficient for the catalyst calendar — but does NOT abort the module, since "no proven catalyst yet" is itself a valid, decision-useful result.

1.name: catalyst-calendar
1.description: Builds the dated 12-month catalyst calendar across all categories (earnings, capital allocation, M&A, refinancing, regulatory/legal, policy, operational, governance, market-structure, cycle), aggregating evidence from the data pool and every upstream module. For each catalyst it records the date/window, why it matters, the evidence it exists, the bullish trigger, the bearish trigger, and whether the timing is proven or vague — the bottom-up implementation of CLAUDE.md §17.

2.name: catalyst-synthesis
2.description: Reads the catalyst module's specialist outputs and produces the final module report — Abstract, Verdict block (with 5 scores and a catalyst verdict), the consolidated 12-month calendar, Score Cap application, Note to Final Synthesizer, and Simple Summary. The master synthesizer reads this output and DEFERS its "Catalyst Calendar" (§7) section to it.

masterSynthesizer.name: synthesizer
masterSynthesizer.description: Reads all specialist outputs in the current run folder and produces a final buy-side thesis. Invoked by the /research:full command after all specialists complete.

balance-sheet-survival.name: Balance Sheet Survival Synthesis
balance-sheet-survival.verdict: Adequate — leverage is elevated (GAAP-built net ~5.8–6.1x) but serviceable; the near-term wall is refinanceable on committed bank capacity in most markets; covenants are balance-sheet only and not earnings-linked, so an earnings shock does…

business-model.name: Value Chain
business-model.verdict: Squeezed — price-taker on inputs AND on outputs.

business-model.name: Business Model Synthesis
business-model.verdict: Cyclical business — worth deeper work only with a strong timing edge

earnings.name: Beat Miss Setup
earnings.verdict: Setup favors beat — narrowly, and only on the quarter, not on the guide.

earnings.name: Earnings Synthesis
earnings.verdict: Mixed earnings setup — conflicting signals: reported revenue acceleration is inorganic (Viterra), GAAP margins compressed for two years while the forward adjusted-spread call is "improving," earnings quality is weak and management-defined,…

management-governance.name: Management Governance Synthesis
management-governance.verdict: Aligned & competent (with a watch flag — the per-share payoff of the flagship acquisition is unproven, and a ~34% blockholder overhang exists)

business-model.name: Moat
business-model.verdict: Narrow moat — and economically unproven so far.

business-model.name: Business Model Synthesis
business-model.verdict: Average business — worth deeper work only if valuation is cheap

earnings.name: Beat Miss Setup
earnings.verdict: Setup is balanced — with the beat/miss skew differing by line: revenue/EBITDA modestly miss-tilted, bottom-line EPS miss-tilted, but the EPS bar is freshly reset lower.

earnings.name: Earnings Synthesis
earnings.verdict: Mixed earnings setup — accelerating at EBITDA, decelerating and distorted at PAT/EPS, with a fair (not soft) consensus bar and net-negative EPS revisions.

management-governance.name: Candor And Disclosure Quality
management-governance.verdict: mixed. Management can be trusted to disclose *operating* bad news plainly — the Andhra Pradesh scheme strike was volunteered with a clean ex-AP growth figure, and the Q4 revenue miss was answered specifically — and every adjustment is disc…

valuation.name: Valuation Synthesis
valuation.verdict: Modestly overvalued *(base-case fair value ~₹575 sits ~11% below the ₹646.15 price; three of four value-producing methods and the reverse-DCF land at-to-below price)*

BG.suggested_action: Do not buy at the indicative ~$123. Track. Re-rate to 'Starter Position Only' only on a pool-confirmed price below ~$100, or on a clean post-Viterra FY2026 cash-conversion print.
BG.sector_benchmark: S&P 500 Consumer Staples / agribusiness peers (ADM, Wilmar)
BG.rating_cap: Policy/commodity-conditional thesis with weak company-specific edge caps at 'Starter Position Only'; negative probability-weighted risk/reward at the indicative price pulls the rating below that to Watchlist. Valuation confidence held at 48 by the no-pool-price gap.
BG.variant_perception_summary: No proven variant perception. The two modest edges (FY2026 back-half consensus may be set too high; the EV/EBITDA discount to ADM is warranted rather than a bargain) are inferences from the filings, not a proprietary insight, and the decisive cross-checks (pool price, peer comps, standalone crush margin) are missing. This is a quality-and-valuation skepticism call backed by filings, not a differentiated view the market cannot see.
BG.what_everyone_knows: Bunge is an ABCD oligopoly crusher; it bought Viterra in July 2025; FY2026 adjusted-EPS guidance was raised ~20% (to $9.00-9.50) on the EPA biofuel decision and an improving crush spread; the balance sheet levered up sharply on the deal.
BG.what_is_priced_in: At the indicative ~$123 price the market prices only ~2.3% FCFF growth and a ~3.3% mid-cycle EBIT margin (reverse-DCF) -- the recovery merely holding at mid-cycle, not recovering to the FY23 peak. The Street's $9.43 already sits above the $9.25 guide midpoint, so the easy upside is arbitraged away.
BG.what_market_may_be_missing: The back half of FY2026 consensus may be too high (full-year estimates rose on H1/Q2 marks while FQ3/FQ4 breadth is net negative), and the 'cheap' EV/EBITDA discount to ADM is warranted by higher leverage, thinner margins and sub-cost-of-capital returns (a flagged value trap), not a mispricing.
BG.killer_risk: One uncontrollable external variable -- biofuel mandate policy via the crush spread -- dominates earnings; a single EPA RVO decision swung a full quarter's adjusted-EPS guidance >100%, with symmetric downside and no contractual margin floor.
kill_criteria: Biofuel-policy or crush-spread reversal retracing FY2026 guidance toward $7.50-8.00 (the Critical earnings flag); H2 spread fades and the year lands at/below the bottom of the $9.00-9.50 range with FQ3/FQ4 breadth still negative.
FY2026 cash conversion stays broken: CFO/EBITDA 25-40%, FCF negative, dividend uncovered again.
A confirmed pool price materially above ~$115 with no change in earnings power (moves the call from Watchlist toward Avoid).
A Viterra goodwill impairment or measurement-period write-down (RF-CAP-001).
2028 maturity-cluster refinancing stress or a downgrade out of investment grade on the ~56% floating-rate book.
Brazil indirect-tax claims (~$760M, rising >$115M/qtr) moving from 'possible' to 'probable'.
Glencore/CPP/BCI ~34% bloc using board seats / supermajority weight against minority holders, or non-arm's-length Glencore/CPP related-party dealing (governance escalation to Critical).

forecast_ledger.prediction: FY2026 full-year adjusted EPS lands inside (not above) the $9.00-9.50 guide.
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Street consensus $9.43 sits at the guide midpoint; FY26 revenue revisions 4 down/1 up; FQ3/FQ4 breadth net negative.
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: Q2/Q3 FY2026 prints plus the Feb-2027 FY result landing inside the $9.00-9.50 range.
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: FY2026 adjusted EPS prints above $9.50 or below $9.00.

forecast_ledger.prediction: Q2 FY2026 beats on the quarter but management does not raise the full-year guide.
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: 5 straight quarterly adjusted-EPS beats; back-half caution embedded in net-negative FQ3/FQ4 breadth.
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: A Q2 adjusted-EPS beat accompanied by an unchanged FY guide.
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: A Q2 miss, or a guide raise above $9.50.

forecast_ledger.prediction: FY2026 free cash flow turns positive / CFO-to-EBITDA recovers toward 70%.
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Q1 is reliably cash-negative; CFO/EBITDA collapsed to 38% (FY25)/25% (TTM); Viterra working-capital build should unwind over the year.
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: FY2026 CFO exceeds capex plus dividend (positive FCF).

forecast_ledger.prediction: At the indicative ~$123 price, BG's 12-month total return is negative (<=0%).
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Probability-weighted expected return approx -11.5% vs the indicative price; no margin of safety (base FV ~$108); negative risk/reward (-0.37).
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: BG trades below ~$123 at the 365-day review.
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: BG sustainably above ~$123 on a held or expanded crush/biofuel spread.

forecast_ledger.prediction: No Viterra goodwill impairment in FY2026.
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: $2,659M preliminary goodwill; integration <1 year old; year-1 synergies >$70M realized.
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: FY2026 10-K passes goodwill impairment tests with no write-down.
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: An impairment or measurement-period write-down disclosed.

forecast_ledger.prediction: No covenant breach and no downgrade out of investment grade in the next 12 months.
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Adequate solvency verdict; covenants are balance-sheet-only (not earnings-linked); IG ratings affirmed post-Viterra; ~$10.5bn committed liquidity.
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: 12 months elapse with no covenant breach, waiver, or sub-IG downgrade.
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: A covenant breach/waiver or a downgrade out of investment grade.

business-model.verdict: Cyclical business -- worth deeper work only with a strong timing edge (business quality 28/100; ROIC 6.7% < 7.2% cost of equity)

earnings.verdict: Mixed earnings setup -- policy-conditional; Material concerns; do not tag accelerating

balance-sheet-survival.verdict: Adequate -- ~5.8-6.1x GAAP-built net leverage; survives -30/-60% EBITDA with no covenant breach; breaks only on a deep inventory write-down

management-governance.verdict: Aligned & competent, on probation -- Governance Rating Watchlist; Governance Score 56; 0 Critical red flags; ~34% Glencore/CPP/BCI bloc overhang

valuation.verdict: Modestly overvalued -- base FV ~$108 vs indicative ~$123; no margin of safety; value-trap flag on the bull case

red_flags.description: Earnings dominated by one uncontrollable external variable (biofuel mandate policy / crush spread); a single EPA RVO decision swung a full quarter's adjusted-EPS guidance >100%, symmetric downside, no contractual margin floor. (Module severity verdict: Material concerns -- conviction haircut, not disqualification.)

Viterra acquisition ($10.6bn = 175% of 3-yr CFO) per-share payoff unproven; ~44% dilution + $7.8bn debt step-up for a >$70M year-1 synergy down-payment; $2.66bn preliminary goodwill (watch -- rationale disclosed, not vague).
Buybacks not reducing share count -- FY2025 share count rose ~44% (133.96M->193.41M); repurchases offset only ~10% of the Viterra issuance.
Dividend uncovered by free cash flow in FY2025 (dividend $459M vs FCF -$879M); covered 5.7x in FY23 and 1.4x in FY24.

Recurring 'one-off' adjustment -- 'Viterra integration' cost adjusted out of non-GAAP earnings for a 3rd straight year ($114M FY23 / $244M FY24 / $223M FY25, +$0.20 EPS Q1'26); treats an active-strategy cost as one-time.

~34% Glencore/CPP/BCI Viterra-seller bloc with contractual rights to name up to four directors and 2/3-supermajority swing weight on mergers/dilutive issuances; the 10-K says it 'could discourage' a value-creating change of control. Structural overhang, not current abuse.

Brazil compounding risk node (severity 62) -- ~$760M unprovisioned indirect-tax claims rising ~$115M/qtr vs ~$816M FY25 net income, plus a farmer-financing book ($835M + $183M, +75%) and Level 3 marks, all in one jurisdiction worsening in the same weak-crop/weak-real scenario.

Net leverage ~5.8-6.1x GAAP-built (up from ~1.3x a year earlier) with negative FCF (-$879M FY25, -$1,161M TTM); refinancing-dependent, not self-funded; 2028 maturity cluster ($2.76bn term loans + $3.5bn revolver expiry); ~56% floating-rate; covenant thresholds undisclosed (headroom Not assessable).

No margin of safety at the indicative ~$123 price (base FV ~$108); bull case (~$140) requires an unearned re-rating on sub-cost-of-capital returns -- a flagged value trap; methods disagree ~53% (forward-normalized vs through-cycle).

missing_data: Pool-sourced current price as of ~2026-06-01 (single highest-value item -- converts every up/downside figure from indicative to pool-grade and decides Watchlist vs Starter; current ~$123.35 is web-sourced/unverified).
Peer margin/ROIC comps export (ADM, Ingredion, Wilmar, other agri-processors) to confirm whether BG's EV/EBITDA discount is warranted.
Clean post-Viterra FY2026 cash-conversion print (CFO/EBITDA, FCF).
Standalone soybean crush margin per ton, processing-only, split from merchandising.
2026 DEF 14A (CD&A + LTIP metric weights, beneficial-ownership table, formal board independence, director RPT, audit fees).
Numeric covenant thresholds (current ratio / debt-to-cap) and a credit-rating-agency report (Moody's/S&P/Fitch).
Debt maturity ladder with weighted-average coupon and floating/fixed detail.
Organic (ex-Viterra) revenue/volume -- first cleanly visible Q3 2026.
BG.notes: Current price is indicative/web-sourced ($123.35, MarketBeat last close 2026-05-29), NOT pool-verified; entry_price is null and no paper trade is created. Watchlist (not Avoid) precisely because the price is unverified -- a pool-confirmed price below ~$100 would re-open a margin of safety and move the call toward 'Starter Position Only', while a pool price above ~$115 would move it toward Avoid. All five planned modules completed; no module overridden; the five syntheses are mutually consistent. No Critical governance/solvency/accounting/going-concern red flag (governance 0 Critical; solvency 'Adequate', not 'Distress'; earnings 'Material concerns' = conviction haircut). Thesis classified commodity/policy-conditional with no proven company-specific edge. expected_return_pct/downside_risk_pct/risk_reward are computed against the indicative price and carry that caveat. The 30d review (2026-07-01) precedes the late-July Q2 FY2026 print; the 90d review (2026-08-30) is the first to capture it.

HCG.suggested_action: Do not buy at INR 646.15. Put on a price-trigger watch; only re-underwrite at/below INR 520 (base-case low, where a margin of safety begins) or on two consecutive clean prints showing ROIC rising toward the cost of capital.
HCG.entry_price_source: NSE last close, Capital IQ EstimatesReport.xls 'Latest Price/Last Close Price' (NSEI:HCG); reconciled vs CIQ Current-Capitalization INR 637.25; both pool-sourced
HCG.rating_cap: Governance 'Weak' (7 High flags, per-share value destroyed) caps headline at Watchlist-or-lower; valuation (modestly overvalued, no margin of safety, negative expected return) carries it to Avoid. No hard verdict-lock (0 Critical flags, no solvency Distress, no hard disqualifier).
HCG.variant_perception_summary: No proven variant perception on the long side. The market already prices the 'cheap EBITDA multiple', and the engine agrees the discount is deserved, not opportunity: the -32% EV/EBITDA discount to peers flips to a +9% EV/EBIT and +64% NTM P/E premium once HCG's weak profit conversion is priced (D&A INR 2,442M, finance cost INR 1,766M, net margin 0.5%, ROIC ~4.6% vs ~11-12% cost of capital for six years). The price implies ~33% FCF CAGR for a decade that HCG has never produced (FCF has fallen INR 1,184M -> INR 586M, FY23-FY26). The engine's only edge is refusing to mistake a deserved discount for a bargain.
HCG.what_everyone_knows: Largest Indian pure-play oncology chain; cancer demand structurally rising and non-deferrable; revenue compounded ~20% for five years; KKR took control (50.84%) and is deleveraging; stock trades at a ~32% EV/EBITDA discount to listed hospital peers.
HCG.what_is_priced_in: EBITDA growth, deleveraging, the KKR-sponsor halo, AND a decade of margin expansion. At INR 646.15 the price implies ~33% FCF growth per year for ten years; the maturity-migration story is already paid for.
HCG.what_market_may_be_missing: That the EBITDA discount is deserved, not opportunity - it evaporates into a premium at EBIT and EPS because HCG converts a rupee of EBITDA into far less profit than peers; and that reported FCF has been falling, not building toward the implied 33% CAGR. (This is agreement with the market's discount, sharpened - not a contrarian long edge.)
HCG.killer_risk: Valuation re-rating failure: the market already pays ~33%-FCF-CAGR-for-a-decade pricing for a business whose FCF has fallen; if the centre-maturity migration disappoints, the multiple de-rates and the equity falls toward the central bear ~INR 440.
kill_criteria: ROIC crosses the cost of capital (~11-12%) as ramping centres mature - would break the Avoid (upgrade). Monitor: annual ROIC, mature-cohort revenue share, centre-cohort ROCE in FY26 audited AR + FY27 prints.
Reported (audited) FY26 PAT reconciles up to the ~INR 557M adjusted figure - earnings de-risk. Monitor: FY26 audited income statement.
EPS revisions turn net-positive plus a clean FQ1 beat - the structural below-EBITDA miss pattern breaks. Monitor: CIQ revisions; FQ1 FY27 print 30-Jul-2026.
Price falls to <= INR 520 - a margin of safety reappears, re-underwrite. Monitor: market price.

DEEPENS AVOID: DPCO / government-scheme tariff tightening on the ~33% policy-exposed revenue - compresses a thin earnings base toward bear INR 440. Monitor: DPCO notifications, state-scheme circulars.
DEEPENS AVOID: a disclosed lender coverage covenant near the assumed ~2.0x - a real break at ~-24% EBITDA, solvency to 'Stretched'. Monitor: FY26 audited debt note / loan terms.
DEEPENS AVOID: another CFO/auditor exit, or Milann closes without an independent fairness opinion - governance deteriorates. Monitor: FY26 AR; FY27 transcripts; Reg-31.
DEEPENS AVOID: the ~INR 3,318M minority puts crystallise into a tight credit window, forcing leverage onto a thin balance sheet. Monitor: FY26 contingencies note; debt-balance trend vs capex.

forecast_ledger.prediction: HCG trades below INR 646.15 (the decision price) in 12 months
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Price ~11% above base fair value ~INR 575; no margin of safety (-12.4%); probability-weighted expected return -13.4%; probability-weighted target INR 559.8
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: Price < INR 646.15 on the 2027-06-01 review date
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: Price >= INR 646.15 sustained at the review date

forecast_ledger.prediction: FY27 EPS-Normalized consensus is NOT raised net-positive over the next 6 months
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: FY27 EPS-N cut -19% over 6 months (-7.5% last month); net revision breadth 2 up / 4 down; EPS missed consensus 3 of last 4 FYs
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: CIQ FY27 EPS-N flat or lower with net breadth <= 0
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: CIQ FY27 EPS-N revised up >5% with positive revision breadth

forecast_ledger.prediction: FQ1 FY27 revenue misses the ~INR 7,340.9M (~+20% implied) consensus bar
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: +11.5% Q4 FY26 exit rate vs a ~+20% YoY implied FQ1 bar; intra-year deceleration +16.6% Q1 -> +11.5% Q4

forecast_ledger.prediction: Group statutory ROIC stays below the ~11-12% cost of capital in FY27
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Statutory Return on Capital ~4.6% for six years; blended ROCE 14% vs a 20% target; mature 27% / ramping 9% / emerging -22% cohort ladder
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: FY27 audited ROIC still single-digit / below WACC

forecast_ledger.prediction: Reported (audited) FY26 PAT does NOT reconcile up to the ~INR 557M adjusted figure cleanly
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: +304.7% adjusted-vs-reported PAT gap; recurring labour-code add-back (INR 127M); auditor Rule 11(g) edit-log finding; reported FY26 PAT INR 138M
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: Audited PAT materially below INR 557M after stripping genuinely recurring items
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: Audited PAT ~= INR 557M with only genuine non-recurring exceptionals

forecast_ledger.prediction: No further CFO/auditor exit before the next governance review
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Three CFOs in ~33 months; near-total board/C-suite churn across two control changes; controls function (auditor/CompSec) clean and continuous
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: No new CFO/auditor resignation disclosed in the window
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: A new CFO or statutory-auditor resignation disclosed

business-model.verdict: Average business - worth deeper work only if valuation is cheap; no disqualifier triggered

earnings.verdict: Mixed earnings setup - accelerating at EBITDA, decelerating and distorted at PAT/EPS; red-flag agent 'Material concerns'

balance-sheet-survival.verdict: Adequate - moderate leverage (2.57x incl. leases), no bullet maturity, equity-funded deleveraging; ICRA A+/A1+

management-governance.verdict: Standard / mixed; Governance Rating 'Weak' (49/100), Confidence-Adjusted 30/100; 7 High / 0 Critical

valuation.verdict: Modestly overvalued with explicit value-trap risk; base FV ~INR 575 vs price INR 646.15; no margin of safety

red_flags.description: Large acquisition with vague rationale (Vizag INR 3,410m, 60.7% goodwill, no disclosed ROI target) + ~INR 3,318m bank-dependent minority put/forward buy-out obligations (INR 1,401.34m already current) management 'cannot assure' it can fund

Promoter-linked RPT: Milann fertility unit sold to Inviga (a fund controlled by founder/Non-Executive Chairman Dr. B.S. Ajaikumar); asset impaired INR 319m first; fairness-opinion/competitive-process evidence not in pool
Management commentary omits the numbers: FY26 reported PAT (-69% to INR 138m) and the 4-year EPS-miss pattern omitted from the worst-year narrative; framing leads only on adjusted EBITDA/PAT
Recurring 'one-off' adjustments: labour-code statutory cost (INR 127m, recurs forward) added back to adjusted PAT; +304.7% adjusted-vs-reported PAT gap
Finance-leadership discontinuity: three CFOs in ~33 months (succession-driven) amid near-total C-suite/board churn across two control changes in five years
25.99% of minority shareholders voted against the promoter-funded KMP bonus (>20% dissent threshold)
INR 319m goodwill impairment on the Milann/BACC fertility acquisition (negative return on deployed capital); further goodwill at risk in Indore (INR 416.89m) and CCC (INR 484.52m) CGUs

Earnings red-flag agent verdict 'Material concerns' (0 Critical / 10 High): EBITDA-up/PAT-down divergence; target prices raised while FY27 EPS cut -19%; ~33% of revenue on uncontrollable DPCO/government-scheme tariffs; structural below-EBITDA EPS miss (3 of last 4 FYs); adjusted PAT +305% of reported

Value-trap risk: the -32% EV/EBITDA discount to peers is warranted, not opportunity - it flips to a +9% EV/EBIT and +64% NTM P/E premium once weak profit conversion is priced; price implies ~33% FCF CAGR/decade never achieved; no margin of safety (-12.4%)

~INR 2.8Bn on-demand overdraft with undisclosed committed availability could be called when needed, opening a ~INR 0.6-1.3Bn 12-month gap in a -40%/-60% downside; ~96% of debt floating and largely unhedged; deleveraging was equity-funded not organic

No lender financial maintenance covenant disclosed - covenant headroom Not assessable; an assumed 2.0x lease-inclusive coverage covenant would breach at ~-24% EBITDA (shallower than a recession), but the break-point is a labeled assumption

missing_data: FY2025-26 audited statutory annual report (single highest-value item) - all FY26 figures currently rest on the 19-May-2026 deck and 20-May-2026 transcript (Level-3, not audited Level-5); an audit true-up is an unquantified risk affecting reported PAT, the adjusted-PAT bridge, ROIC, and the maturity schedule

Lender credit agreement / loan-facility terms sheet - covenant thresholds, year-by-year maturity schedule, committed revolver/cash-credit availability, and change-of-control/cross-default triggers (would lift three of the four binding balance-sheet caps at once)

Post-KKR-changeover Reg-31(1) shareholding-and-encumbrance pattern + FY26 Corporate Governance Report - current CEO/CFO (Mattoo/Saxena) pay and metrics, personal stakes, board independence %, occupancy/ARPOB KPI restoration, Vizag CGU ROCE, Milann fairness opinion, and the KKR-block pledge line

Centre-level utilisation / ROCE disclosure by maturity cohort - to test whether the ramping centres are actually migrating into the mature ~27%-ROCE bucket (the entire bull case); per-centre payback/vintage curve not disclosed

Name-specific (non-vendor-distorted) beta to tighten WACC; a listed pure-play oncology comp (Apollo's oncology vertical and private AOI do not disclose stand-alone oncology margins, so peer comps use the multi-specialty set)
IBKR options / positioning data - none in pool; no options-vs-stock or hedge construction could be assessed (HCG is a controlled, thin-float NSE name with likely poor single-stock options liquidity)
HCG.notes: Avoid (long), not a high-conviction short: adequate balance sheet + real non-deferrable demand + a live (unproven) centre-maturity ramp + KKR-sponsor optionality make a naked short asymmetric in the wrong direction over 12 months. Basket = Rejected; paper treatment = no trade, track avoided/foregone return (entry-equivalent INR 646.15 recorded for opportunity-cost tracking only, NOT an executed paper short). Current price IS pool-sourced (no web quote), so margin of safety is fully assessable - there is none (-12.4%). The 30d review (2026-07-01) lands just before the FQ1 FY27 print (30-Jul-2026); the FY26 audited annual report is the highest-value catalyst but has no firm public date. Math reconciles: probabilities sum to 100%, probability-weighted target INR 559.8, expected return -13.37% (matches both ways), risk/reward -0.42; the Avoid is robust to reasonable probability re-weighting (positive expected return would require bull case >53% probability, not defensible). No module was overridden - the five modules are mutually consistent. The second-best bet (prefer higher-ROIC multi-specialty operators e.g. Max/Apollo, or a quality-long/HCG-short pair if a short is mandated) is a direction only, not underwritten, as no full valuation work-up on those names is in the pool.
